data.title: Singapore set to remain fastest-growing private wealth hub in next 5 years
data.description:
Bloomberg Intelligence said Singapore “retains a unique edge with deep Southeast Asia connectivity."

Singapore is expected to remain among the world’s fastest-growing cross-border private-wealth hubs over the next five years, with cross-border assets in both centers projected to rise by about 12% annually, according to a new Bloomberg Intelligence (BI) report.

BI said Singapore “retains a unique edge with deep Southeast Asia connectivity,” underscoring its regional pipeline for client flows and deal opportunities.

Across Asia’s private-banking industry, 85% of surveyed senior practitioners expect assets under management and net new money to grow by at least 6% a year over the next five years; roughly a quarter foresee double-digit gains, BI reported.

Respondents expect client acquisition to be driven in part by Mainland China, which could account for 30% of new clients over the next three to five years, compared to 26% currently, with incremental contributions from the Middle East.

Risk appetite appears to be rising: half of respondents said their clients have become more risk-on over the past 12 months, with allocations expected to tilt toward equities, private equity, digital assets, and hedge funds in the year ahead.

Technology was cited as the top driver of future inflows — and the biggest cost item — as firms invest in more integrated systems. BI added that 57% see AI as most improving client-data insights, whilst only 3% expect staff reductions from AI adoption.

data.title: Singapore bond issuance hits record $44.6b in first nine months of 2025
data.description:
DBS Group Holdings, UOB, and DBS Bank each issued $2.59b.

Singapore’s bond market notched its strongest year-to-date on record in the first nine months of 2025, with primary issuance reaching $44.55b—the highest since records began in 1980, according to LSEG’s Investment Banking Review.

Financial institutions dominated supply with $28.88b in new paper (64.6% share). Government & Agencies contributed $8.29b, while Real Estate issuers raised $2.07b.

Among the largest USD prints (shown in SGD, converted), DBS Group Holdings, UOB, and DBS Bank each issued $2.59b, alongside Temasek Financial (I) at $1.94b and OCBC at $1.30b. (Other top deals priced in EUR/AUD are not shown in USD→SGD terms.)

On the DCM league table for Singapore-domiciled bonds, DBS led with $6.09b in credited volume (13.6% share), followed by OCBC ($4.36b) and UOB ($4.13b).

data.title: Singapore bond issuance hits record $44.3b in first nine months of 2025
data.description:
By sector, financial institutions dominated issuance with $28.76b in new supply.

Singapore’s bond market recorded its strongest performance on record in the first nine months of 2025, with primary issuance reaching $44.37b, the highest year-to-date total since records began in 1980, according to LSEG’s Singapore Investment Banking Review.

The surge was driven primarily by corporate issuers, who accounted for $34.57b of the total. Government and agency issuers contributed $8.25b.

By sector, financial institutions dominated issuance with $28.76b in new supply, representing the largest single-sector share. Government and agencies followed with $8.25b, while real estate issuers raised $2.06b.

DBS topped the DCM league table with $6.06b in credited deal volume across 43 issues, capturing a 13.6% market share. OCBC ranked second with $4.35b, followed by UOB with $4.11b

data.title: Singapore equity issuance hits strongest YTD since 2021 at $7.5b raised
data.description:
Sector-wise, Real Estate led with $3.50b in ECM proceeds.

Singapore’s equity capital markets (ECM) raised $7.51b in the first nine months of 2025, a 179% YoY increase, according to LSEG’s Singapore Investment Banking Review.

This marks the strongest year-to-date ECM performance since 2021, driven by a rebound in IPO activity and strong follow-on issuance.

Proceeds in Q3 alone reached $3.24b, down 18.6% from Q2, though the number of equity issues surged 71% YoY.

IPO fundraising soared to $2.72b from 29 deals, nearly 18 times higher than the same period last year. Of these, 22 companies listed offshore—20 in the US and 2 in Hong Kong—raising $755.4m.

The remaining 7 IPOs on the Singapore Exchange (SGX) accounted for $1.94b. According to LSEG, Q3 2025 was the busiest quarter for IPOs on Singapore exchanges since Q3 2017.

Sector-wise, Real Estate led with $3.50b in ECM proceeds, contributing 46.3% of the total. High Technology followed at $2.59b, or 35.4% of the market.

Among bookrunners for Singapore-domiciled equity and equity-linked transactions, DBS led with $856.4m in deal value and an 11.4% market share, followed by J.P. Morgan with $849.1m, and Morgan Stanley with $778.8m.

data.title: Singapore M&A value rises 7.1% to $70.1b in first nine months of 2025
data.description:
In sector rankings, High Technology led with $10.62b in deal value.

Singapore’s mergers and acquisitions (M&A) activity hit $70.06b in the first nine months of 2025, rising 7.1% YoY, according to LSEG’s Investment Banking Review.

The increase came despite a 25% decline in deal count, which fell to a nine-year low. A total of 14 deals exceeding $1.68b (US$1b) were announced, collectively amounting to $29.78b, with none surpassing $3.89b (US$3b).

By deal direction, target-Singapore activity surged 31.3% to $29.53b, the highest among all categories. Inbound transactions rose 38.4% to $21.89b, whilst domestic M&A increased 14.4% to $7.64b. Outbound deals, however, slipped 2.6% to $18.13b, reaching a decade low.

In sector rankings, High Technology led with $10.62b in deal value, accounting for 15.2% of the total and marking a 54.1% YoY increase. Real Estate also posted $10.62b, followed by Energy & Power at $8.68b.

Among advisers, Morgan Stanley led for M&A involving any Singapore entity, advising on $5.70b in announced deals and securing an 8.2% market share, according to LSEG’s rankings.

data.title: Singlife partners Fullerton to expand investment offerings on GROW
data.description:
GROW will serve as a key wealth platform for Fullerton’s investment solutions.

Singlife and its investment platform GROW has announced a preferred partnership with Fullerton Fund Management.

Singlife will be able to offer its customers and financial advisers' access to Fullerton solutions across ILPs and its investment platforms GROW and dollarDEX. GROW will play a central role in this partnership, serving as a key wealth platform for Fullerton’s investment solutions, according to the press release.

Meanwhile, Fullerton will leverage Singlife’s established distribution network across B2B and D2C channels to offer investment opportunities.

This partnership with Fullerton strengthens Singlife’s unique ability to provide holistic solutions both in insurance and investments, said Helen Shen, group head of products, Singlife.

“By marrying Fullerton’s investment expertise with Singlife’s trusted distribution channels, we are creating powerful synergy that allows us to deliver better outcomes for our customers,” Shen said.

data.title: Gamuda wins $1.01b land tender in Singapore
data.description:
It will comprise up to 875 residential units.

Gamuda Bhd has secured a Housing Development Board (HDB) land parcel in Singapore worth $1.012b (RM3.31b).

The tender, awarded on 26 September and accepted on 29 September, was submitted by Gamuda’s wholly owned subsidiary Gamuda (Singapore) Pte Ltd together with Evia MCS Pte Ltd and H108 Pte Ltd.

The land will be acquired through joint-venture companies Polaris Times Square Residences Pte Ltd and Times Square Mall Pte Ltd.

The proposed development will comprise up to 875 residential units and 135,625 sq ft of commercial space, alongside a bus interchange, retail facilities, and a hawker centre.

Gamuda said the project is expected to contribute positively to earnings and shareholder value, with completion targeted within 84 months.

data.title: Singapore’s private banking industry seeks to strengthen philanthropy advisory
data.description:
They want to enhance advisors’ capabilities related to making philanthropic decisions.

The Wealth Management Institute (WMI) has partnered with the Private Banking Industry Group (PBIG) to advise capabilities and explore ways to elevate Singapore’s position as a hub for effective philanthropy.

The new phase of collaboration focuses on enhancing the capabilities of client-facing advisors who are the first point of contact for principals, wealth owners, and their families.

The goal is to better meet the growing demand for values-based philanthropy advisory that resonates with the next generation—who are often more attuned to social and environmental causes, the WMI and PBIG said in a press release.

Since 2022, WMI’s philanthropy programs have achieved about 600 enrolments. These programmes aim to equip advisors in guiding clients towards making more effective philanthropic decisions.

New programmes have also been planned, including a Certificate in Philanthropy and Social Impact for Greater China Markets to be launched in November 2025.

data.title: Aman to open first Singapore property at The Skywaters
data.description:
It will include a hotel and a limited number of branded residences.

Luxury hospitality brand Aman will open its first property in Singapore at The Skywaters, set to be the city’s tallest skyscraper.

The project will include a hotel, a limited number of branded residences, and an exclusive private members’ club.

Developed by a consortium led by Perennial Holdings, with design by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill, The Skywaters is located at the intersection of Singapore’s Central Business District, Marina Bay, and the future Greater Southern Waterfront.

Aman Singapore will feature interiors by Kerry Hill Architects. The property will include a floor of large hotel suites with views of the city and the South China Sea, an Aman Club, and a collection of residences ranging from one to five bedrooms.

Facilities will be reserved for residents, members, and guests, and will include an Aman Spa spanning an entire floor, an infinity pool, and several dining venues featuring global and signature Aman concepts.

The branded residences will provide full access to Aman services and amenities, offering a private retreat above Singapore’s cityscape.

data.title: Nintendo sets up Singapore subsidiary to drive SEA growth
data.description:
It has a capital stock of $8m.

Nintendo Co., Ltd. has established Nintendo Singapore Pte. Ltd. on 26 September 2025, as part of its expansion in Southeast Asia.

The new subsidiary, fully owned by Nintendo, has a capital stock of $8m and will focus on accelerating the company’s regional business.

The office will be led by Managing Director Takahiro Miura.

Nintendo also said it is considering creating a similar entity in Thailand.
